title: Mean Squared Error Loss
description:
Implement the mean squared error between a prediction vector and a target vector $\text{pred}, \text{target} \in \mathbb{R}^n$:
$$\text{MSE}(\text{pred}, \text{target}) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n} (\text{pred}_i - \text{target}_i)^2$$

Implement `mse(pred, target)` returning a single scalar.

**Shapes**: `pred`, `target`: `(8,)` -> returns a scalar.
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def mse(pred, target):
    # TODO: mean((pred - target) ** 2)
    pass
hints: Compute the elementwise difference (pred - target), square it, then take the mean over all elements.
np.mean((pred - target) ** 2) handles both the squaring and the averaging in one expression.

solutions.name: Mean of squared elementwise differences
solutions.explanation: MSE penalizes the average squared deviation between predictions and targets; squaring makes all errors non-negative and weights larger errors more heavily, and dividing by $n$ normalizes for vector length so the loss is comparable across batch sizes.
